analysis.consistency: High. The spell description is straightforward and aligns with the mechanics implied by its name (a stronger version of 'Invisibility').
analysis.quality: Good. The description is concise and clearly defines the effect, including the crucial detail that worn or carried gear becomes invisible along with the target.

mechanics.effect: Grants invisibility to the target (self or touched creature). Gear worn or carried is also turned invisible.
mechanics.notes: This spell is mechanically superior to the 2nd-level 'Invisibility' as it does not require the target to drop what they are carrying for it to also become invisible, and it is a higher level spell, suggesting a more robust or permanent effect (though both are concentration). The link to the 'invisible' condition is standard practice.
